// Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot from typed DSLX AOT package "native_dslx_tests".

pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{AllZeros, ExecutionOptions, Token};
pub type U1 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<1>;
pub type U2 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<2>;
pub type U4 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<4>;
pub type U8 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU8<8>;
pub type U16 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU16<16>;
pub type U23 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedBitsInU32<23>;
pub type U65 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::UnsignedWideBits<65, 2>;
pub type S2 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::SignedBitsInU8<2>;
pub type S4 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::SignedBitsInU8<4>;
pub type S65 = xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::SignedWideBits<65, 2>;

#[repr(C)]
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct XlsynthPirAotTuple0 {
    pub field0: U8,
    pub field1: U16,
}
impl XlsynthPirAotTuple0 {
    /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
    pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
        Self {
            field0: <U8 as AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            field1: <U16 as AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
        }
    }
}
impl AllZeros for XlsynthPirAotTuple0 {
    fn all_zeros() -> Self {
        XlsynthPirAotTuple0::all_zeros()
    }
}


pub mod bar {
    pub mod widget {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        #[repr(C)]
        #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
        pub struct Widget {
            pub widget_id: super::super::U8,
        }
        impl Widget {
            /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
            pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
                Self {
                    widget_id: <super::super::U8 as super::super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                }
            }
        }
        impl super::super::AllZeros for Widget {
            fn all_zeros() -> Self {
                Widget::all_zeros()
            }
        }

    }
    pub mod your_file {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        pub mod aot_namespaced_package_bump {
            #![allow(dead_code)]

            #![allow(unused_imports)]

            use super::*;

            // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

            pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
            use std::sync::LazyLock;
            #[allow(unused_imports)]
            use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
                AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
                ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
            };

            unsafe extern "C" {
                #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_namespaced_package_bump"]
                fn linked_entrypoint(
                    inputs: *const *const u8,
                    output: *mut u8,
                    scratch: *mut u8,
                    context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
                ) -> i32;
            }

            const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 41;
            const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
            static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
                LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

            fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
                std::hint::black_box([
                    xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
                ]);
            }

            /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
            pub struct Runner {
                scratch: Vec<u64>,
                context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
            }

            impl Runner {
                /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
                pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                    ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                    if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                        )));
                    }
                    Ok(Self {
                        scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                        context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                    })
                }

                fn invoke(
                    &mut self,
                    inputs: &[*const u8],
                    output: *mut u8,
                    options: ExecutionOptions,
                ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                    let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                        std::ptr::null_mut()
                    } else {
                        self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                    };
                    let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                    let status = unsafe {
                        linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                    };
                    if status == 0 {
                        Ok(())
                    } else {
                        Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                    }
                }

                fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                            failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                        )));
                    }
                    if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                            failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                        )));
                    }
                    Ok(())
                }

                /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
                pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, doodle: &super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, output: &mut super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                    let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(doodle).cast::<u8>()];
                    self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                    Ok(self.context.result())
                }

                /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
                pub fn run(&mut self, doodle: &super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, output: &mut super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(doodle).cast::<u8>()];
                    self.invoke(
                        &input_pointers,
                        std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                        ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                    )?;
                    Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
                }
            }

            /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
            pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
                Runner::new()
            }

            std::thread_local! {
                static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                    const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
            }

            /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
            pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
                f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
            ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
                THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                    let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                    if runner_slot.is_none() {
                        runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                    }
                    Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
                })
            }

            /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
            pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
                f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
            ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
                with_thread_local_runner(f)?
            }

        }
    }
}
pub mod events {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    pub mod aot_events {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_events"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 97;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 88, kind: EventKind::Trace, label: None, message: None, format: Some("accepted x={}".to_string()), verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 8, byte_count: 1 }] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 94, kind: EventKind::Cover, label: Some("accepted".to_string()), message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 92, kind: EventKind::Assert, label: Some("bad_condition".to_string()), message: Some("Assertion failure via assert! @ src/events.x:6:12-6:33".to_string()), format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 87, kind: EventKind::Trace, label: None, message: None, format: Some("x={} y={:x}".to_string()), verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 8, byte_count: 1 }, TraceValueLayout::Bits { bit_count: 8, byte_count: 1 }] }, EventSiteMetadata { node_text_id: 93, kind: EventKind::Cover, label: Some("covered".to_string()), message: None, format: None, verbosity: 0, operand_layouts: vec![] }] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, x: &super::super::U8, y: &super::super::U8, ok: &super::super::U1, emit: &super::super::U1, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(x).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(y).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(ok).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(emit).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod foo {
    pub mod my_file {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        pub mod aot_namespaced_package_echo {
            #![allow(dead_code)]

            #![allow(unused_imports)]

            use super::*;

            // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

            pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
            use std::sync::LazyLock;
            #[allow(unused_imports)]
            use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
                AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
                ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
            };

            unsafe extern "C" {
                #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_namespaced_package_echo"]
                fn linked_entrypoint(
                    inputs: *const *const u8,
                    output: *mut u8,
                    scratch: *mut u8,
                    context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
                ) -> i32;
            }

            const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 33;
            const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
            static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
                LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

            fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
                std::hint::black_box([
                    xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                    xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
                ]);
            }

            /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
            pub struct Runner {
                scratch: Vec<u64>,
                context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
            }

            impl Runner {
                /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
                pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                    ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                    if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                        )));
                    }
                    Ok(Self {
                        scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                        context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                    })
                }

                fn invoke(
                    &mut self,
                    inputs: &[*const u8],
                    output: *mut u8,
                    options: ExecutionOptions,
                ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                    let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                        std::ptr::null_mut()
                    } else {
                        self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                    };
                    let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                    let status = unsafe {
                        linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                    };
                    if status == 0 {
                        Ok(())
                    } else {
                        Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                    }
                }

                fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                            failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                        )));
                    }
                    if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                        return Err(RunError(format!(
                            "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                            failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                        )));
                    }
                    Ok(())
                }

                /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
                pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, doodle: &super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, output: &mut super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                    let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(doodle).cast::<u8>()];
                    self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                    Ok(self.context.result())
                }

                /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
                pub fn run(&mut self, doodle: &super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle, output: &mut super::super::super::types::shared_types::Doodle) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                    let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(doodle).cast::<u8>()];
                    self.invoke(
                        &input_pointers,
                        std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                        ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                    )?;
                    Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
                }
            }

            /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
            pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
                Runner::new()
            }

            std::thread_local! {
                static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                    const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
            }

            /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
            pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
                f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
            ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
                THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                    let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                    if runner_slot.is_none() {
                        runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                    }
                    Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
                })
            }

            /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
            pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
                f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
            ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
                with_thread_local_runner(f)?
            }

        }
    }
    pub mod widget {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        #[repr(C)]
        #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
        pub struct Widget {
            pub widget_id: super::super::U8,
        }
        impl Widget {
            /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
            pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
                Self {
                    widget_id: <super::super::U8 as super::super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                }
            }
        }
        impl super::super::AllZeros for Widget {
            fn all_zeros() -> Self {
                Widget::all_zeros()
            }
        }

    }
}
pub mod frobber {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    pub mod aot_duplicate_widget {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_duplicate_widget"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 41;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, widget: &super::super::foo::widget::Widget, output: &mut super::super::bar::widget::Widget, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(widget).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, widget: &super::super::foo::widget::Widget, output: &mut super::super::bar::widget::Widget) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(widget).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod gizmo_types {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    #[repr(transparent)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct GizmoMode(pub super::U2);
    impl GizmoMode {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self(<super::U2 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros())
        }
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Idle: Self = Self(super::U2::from_raw_bits(0));
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Frob: Self = Self(super::U2::from_raw_bits(1));
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Quux: Self = Self(super::U2::from_raw_bits(2));
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for GizmoMode {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            GizmoMode::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(transparent)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct SignedGizmoMode(pub super::S2);
    impl SignedGizmoMode {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self(<super::S2 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros())
        }
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Negative: Self = Self(super::S2::from_raw_bits(3));
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Neutral: Self = Self(super::S2::from_raw_bits(0));
        #[allow(non_upper_case_globals)]
        pub const Positive: Self = Self(super::S2::from_raw_bits(1));
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for SignedGizmoMode {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            SignedGizmoMode::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    pub type FrobNibbles = [super::U4; 3];

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct GizmoTuning {
        pub frob_bias: super::U4,
        pub wobble_trim: super::S4,
    }
    impl GizmoTuning {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                frob_bias: <super::U4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                wobble_trim: <super::S4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for GizmoTuning {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            GizmoTuning::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct Gizmo {
        pub gizmo_id: super::U8,
        pub mode: GizmoMode,
        pub frobs: [super::U4; 3],
        pub tuning: GizmoTuning,
        pub wobble: super::S4,
        pub signed_mode: SignedGizmoMode,
    }
    impl Gizmo {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                gizmo_id: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                mode: <GizmoMode as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                frobs: <[super::U4; 3] as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                tuning: <GizmoTuning as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                wobble: <super::S4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                signed_mode: <SignedGizmoMode as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for Gizmo {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Gizmo::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct GizmoOutcome {
        pub next_gizmo_id: super::U8,
        pub selected_frob: super::U4,
        pub mode: GizmoMode,
        pub adjusted_wobble: super::S4,
        pub signed_mode: SignedGizmoMode,
    }
    impl GizmoOutcome {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                next_gizmo_id: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                selected_frob: <super::U4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                mode: <GizmoMode as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                adjusted_wobble: <super::S4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                signed_mode: <SignedGizmoMode as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for GizmoOutcome {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            GizmoOutcome::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    pub mod aot_gizmo_frob {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_gizmo_frob"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 53;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, gizmo: &super::Gizmo, garnish: &[super::super::U4; 3], output: &mut super::GizmoOutcome, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(gizmo).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(garnish).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, gizmo: &super::Gizmo, garnish: &[super::super::U4; 3], output: &mut super::GizmoOutcome) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(gizmo).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(garnish).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod invokes_and_loop {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    pub mod aot_invokes_and_loop {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_invokes_and_loop"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 41;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, init: &super::super::U8, increment: &super::super::U8, output: &mut super::super::U8) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(init).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(increment).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod parametric_forms {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct Box__N_8 {
        pub value: super::U8,
    }
    impl Box__N_8 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                value: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for Box__N_8 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Box__N_8::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct Box__N_16 {
        pub value: super::U16,
    }
    impl Box__N_16 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                value: <super::U16 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for Box__N_16 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Box__N_16::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct Matrix__R_2__C_3 {
        pub rows: [[super::U8; 3]; 2],
    }
    impl Matrix__R_2__C_3 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                rows: <[[super::U8; 3]; 2] as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for Matrix__R_2__C_3 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Matrix__R_2__C_3::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct ArrayBox__N_4 {
        pub items: [super::U8; 4],
    }
    impl ArrayBox__N_4 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                items: <[super::U8; 4] as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for ArrayBox__N_4 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            ArrayBox__N_4::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct ExprBox__N_8 {
        pub value: super::U8,
    }
    impl ExprBox__N_8 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                value: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for ExprBox__N_8 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            ExprBox__N_8::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct SignedTag__S_m3 {
        pub payload: super::U8,
    }
    impl SignedTag__S_m3 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                payload: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for SignedTag__S_m3 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            SignedTag__S_m3::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct WideTag__W_18446744073709551616 {
        pub payload: super::U8,
    }
    impl WideTag__W_18446744073709551616 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                payload: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for WideTag__W_18446744073709551616 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            WideTag__W_18446744073709551616::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    pub type Box8 = Box__N_8;

    pub type Box16 = Box__N_16;

    pub type Box8Array4 = [Box8; 4];

    pub type Matrix2x3 = Matrix__R_2__C_3;

    pub type ArrayBox4 = ArrayBox__N_4;

    pub type ExprBox8 = ExprBox__N_8;

    pub type NegativeTag = SignedTag__S_m3;

    pub type HugeTag = WideTag__W_18446744073709551616;

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct WidePair {
        pub unsigned_value: super::U65,
        pub signed_value: super::S65,
    }
    impl WidePair {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                unsigned_value: <super::U65 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                signed_value: <super::S65 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for WidePair {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            WidePair::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct ParametricFormsResult {
        pub box8: Box8,
        pub box16: Box16,
        pub matrix: Matrix2x3,
        pub array_box: ArrayBox4,
        pub box_array: [Box8; 4],
        pub expr_box: ExprBox8,
        pub negative: NegativeTag,
        pub huge: HugeTag,
        pub wide_pair: WidePair,
    }
    impl ParametricFormsResult {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                box8: <Box8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                box16: <Box16 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                matrix: <Matrix2x3 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                array_box: <ArrayBox4 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                box_array: <[Box8; 4] as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                expr_box: <ExprBox8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                negative: <NegativeTag as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                huge: <HugeTag as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                wide_pair: <WidePair as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for ParametricFormsResult {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            ParametricFormsResult::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    pub mod aot_parametric_forms {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_parametric_forms"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 208;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, box8: &super::Box8, box16: &super::Box16, matrix: &super::Matrix2x3, array_box: &super::ArrayBox4, box_array: &[super::Box8; 4], expr_box: &super::ExprBox8, negative: &super::NegativeTag, huge: &super::HugeTag, wide_pair: &super::WidePair, output: &mut super::ParametricFormsResult, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(box8).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(box16).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(matrix).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(array_box).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(box_array).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(expr_box).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(negative).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(huge).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(wide_pair).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, box8: &super::Box8, box16: &super::Box16, matrix: &super::Matrix2x3, array_box: &super::ArrayBox4, box_array: &[super::Box8; 4], expr_box: &super::ExprBox8, negative: &super::NegativeTag, huge: &super::HugeTag, wide_pair: &super::WidePair, output: &mut super::ParametricFormsResult) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(box8).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(box16).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(matrix).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(array_box).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(box_array).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(expr_box).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(negative).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(huge).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(wide_pair).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod parametric_imports {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct ParametricImportsResult {
        pub remote: super::parametric_lib::RemotePlain,
        pub imported_direct: super::parametric_lib::RemoteBox__N_8,
        pub imported_pair: super::parametric_lib::RemotePair__A_8__B_23,
    }
    impl ParametricImportsResult {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                remote: <super::parametric_lib::RemotePlain as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                imported_direct: <super::parametric_lib::RemoteBox__N_8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                imported_pair: <super::parametric_lib::RemotePair__A_8__B_23 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for ParametricImportsResult {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            ParametricImportsResult::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    pub mod aot_parametric_imports {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_parametric_imports"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 76;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }

            fn invoke(
                &mut self,
                inputs: &[*const u8],
                output: *mut u8,
                options: ExecutionOptions,
            ) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                self.context.clear_with_options(options);
                let scratch = if self.scratch.is_empty() {
                    std::ptr::null_mut()
                } else {
                    self.scratch.as_mut_ptr().cast::<u8>()
                };
                let mut raw_context = self.context.raw_context();
                let status = unsafe {
                    linked_entrypoint(inputs.as_ptr(), output, scratch, &mut raw_context)
                };
                if status == 0 {
                    Ok(())
                } else {
                    Err(RunError(format!("compiled AOT entrypoint returned status {status}")))
                }
            }

            fn reject_failures(context: &ExecutionContext<'_>) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                if let Some(failure) = context.assertion_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assertion failed at node {}: {}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.message
                    )));
                }
                if let Some(failure) = context.assumption_failures().first() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "compiled assumed-in-bounds condition failed at node {}: {:?}",
                        failure.node_text_id, failure.kind
                    )));
                }
                Ok(())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage and returns observable events.
            pub fn run_with_events(&mut self, remote: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemotePlain, imported_direct: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemoteBox__N_8, imported_pair: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemotePair__A_8__B_23, output: &mut super::ParametricImportsResult, options: ExecutionOptions) -> Result<ExecutionResult, RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(remote).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(imported_direct).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(imported_pair).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(&input_pointers, std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(), options)?;
                Ok(self.context.result())
            }

            /// Runs into caller-owned output storage, rejecting assertion/assumption failures.
            pub fn run(&mut self, remote: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemotePlain, imported_direct: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemoteBox__N_8, imported_pair: &super::super::parametric_lib::RemotePair__A_8__B_23, output: &mut super::ParametricImportsResult) -> Result<(), RunError> {
                let input_pointers = [std::ptr::from_ref(remote).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(imported_direct).cast::<u8>(), std::ptr::from_ref(imported_pair).cast::<u8>()];
                self.invoke(
                    &input_pointers,
                    std::ptr::from_mut(output).cast::<u8>(),
                    ExecutionOptions::NO_EVENTS,
                )?;
                Self::reject_failures(&self.context)
            }
        }

        /// Creates a reusable runner for this native AOT entrypoint.
        pub fn new_runner() -> Result<Runner, RunError> {
            Runner::new()
        }

        std::thread_local! {
            static THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER: std::cell::RefCell<Option<Runner>> =
                const { std::cell::RefCell::new(None) };
        }

        /// Runs a closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> T,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            THREAD_LOCAL_RUNNER.with(|runner_cell| {
                let mut runner_slot = runner_cell.borrow_mut();
                if runner_slot.is_none() {
                    runner_slot.replace(new_runner()?);
                }
                Ok(f(runner_slot.as_mut().expect("runner was initialized")))
            })
        }

        /// Runs a fallible closure with this thread's cached runner.
        pub fn try_with_thread_local_runner<T>(
            f: impl FnOnce(&mut Runner) -> Result<T, RunError>,
        ) -> Result<T, RunError> {
            with_thread_local_runner(f)?
        }

    }
}
pub mod parametric_lib {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct RemoteBox__N_8 {
        pub value: super::U8,
    }
    impl RemoteBox__N_8 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                value: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for RemoteBox__N_8 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            RemoteBox__N_8::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct RemotePair__A_8__B_23 {
        pub left: super::U8,
        pub right: super::U23,
    }
    impl RemotePair__A_8__B_23 {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                left: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
                right: <super::U23 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for RemotePair__A_8__B_23 {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            RemotePair__A_8__B_23::all_zeros()
        }
    }

    #[repr(C)]
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub struct RemotePlain {
        pub id: super::U8,
    }
    impl RemotePlain {
        /// Constructs a value whose DSLX/PIR data bits are all zero.
        pub fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            Self {
                id: <super::U8 as super::AllZeros>::all_zeros(),
            }
        }
    }
    impl super::AllZeros for RemotePlain {
        fn all_zeros() -> Self {
            RemotePlain::all_zeros()
        }
    }

}
pub mod tuple_shapes {
    #![allow(dead_code)]

    #![allow(unused_imports)]

    pub mod aot_tuple_shapes {
        #![allow(dead_code)]

        #![allow(unused_imports)]

        use super::*;

        // Generated by xlsynth_pir_compiler::aot.

        pub use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{ExecutionOptions, ExecutionResult, RunError};
        use std::sync::LazyLock;
        #[allow(unused_imports)]
        use xlsynth_pir_compiler_runtime::{
            AssumptionFailureKind, CompiledFunctionMetadata, EventKind, EventSiteMetadata,
            ExecutionContext, RawExecutionContext, TraceTupleFieldLayout, TraceValueLayout,
            xlsynth_pir_record_assert, xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure,
            xlsynth_pir_record_cover, xlsynth_pir_record_trace,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice, xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp,
            xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op,
        };

        unsafe extern "C" {
            #[link_name = "__xlsynth_pir_aot_tuple_shapes"]
            fn linked_entrypoint(
                inputs: *const *const u8,
                output: *mut u8,
                scratch: *mut u8,
                context: *mut RawExecutionContext,
            ) -> i32;
        }

        const SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT: usize = 52;
        const SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT: usize = 8;
        static FUNCTION_METADATA: LazyLock<CompiledFunctionMetadata> =
            LazyLock::new(|| CompiledFunctionMetadata { event_sites: vec![] });

        fn ensure_runtime_symbols_linked() {
            std::hint::black_box([
                xlsynth_pir_record_assert as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_assumption_failure as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_cover as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_record_trace as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_binop as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_dynamic_bit_slice as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_bit_slice_update as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_unary_op as *const () as usize,
                xlsynth_pir_runtime_wide_mulp as *const () as usize,
            ]);
        }

        /// Reusable runner for the generated native AOT entrypoint.
        pub struct Runner {
            scratch: Vec<u64>,
            context: ExecutionContext<'static>,
        }

        impl Runner {
            /// Creates a runner with reusable scratch storage and an event collector.
            pub fn new() -> Result<Self, RunError> {
                ensure_runtime_symbols_linked();
                if SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT > std::mem::align_of::<u64>() {
                    return Err(RunError(format!(
                        "unsupported AOT scratch alignment: {SCRATCH_ALIGNMENT}"
                    )));
                }
                Ok(Self {
                    scratch: vec![0u64; SCRATCH_BYTE_COUNT.div_ceil(std::mem::size_of::<u64>())],
                    context: ExecutionContext::new(&*FUNCTION_METADATA),
                })
            }
